item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.4 Index, List of Drawings, 1956
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:

pages.page_summary: This page is a detailed index and list of drawings from Topolski's Chronicle Volume IV for the year 1956, covering a wide range of political, cultural, and international subjects including events, locations, and notable figures.

pages.typeset_text: TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE 1956 VOLUME IV INDEX Acton, musicians and other performers ... (Text continues as an alphabetically structured index, listing a range of subjects including people, places, organizations, and topics covered in Volume IV of Topolski's Chronicle for the year 1956. Each entry is followed by page numbers and consists of various categories such as countries, individuals, political parties, professions, artistic and musical figures, various institutions, and notable events. At the top, publication details are prominent, and the rest of the page is densely packed with index listings in typeset form.)

people: Selassie, H.M. The Emperor of Ethiopia
events: Obituary: The Rev. Albert Bullen, Dean of Windsor
Obituary: The Rt. Hon. Sir Stafford Cripps

pages.page_summary: This page is the comprehensive index for Volume IV, 1956, of Topolski's Chronicle, listing people, places, organizations, and notable events covered in that year's biweekly issues. The typeset index serves as a structured gateway to the Chronicle's content but features no sketches or handwritten notes.

LIST OF DRAWINGS

VOLUME I, 1953 :
No. 1 Coronation of H.M. Queen Elizabeth II ; No. 2 Sir Winston Churchill ; No. 3 London ; No. 4 Conferences and Congresses ; No. 5 Spain I ; No. 6 Spain II—Madrid ; No. 7 Spain III ; No. 8 England—County Horse Shows ; No. 9 London Freaks, Misfits, Grotesques, Eccentrics, etc.; No. 10-11 Mid-Century Retrospect ; No. 12 Mother and Child.

VOLUME II, 1954 :
No. 1 (13) London—Bombay ; No. 2 (14) Bombay—India, the countryside ; No. 3 (15) New Delhi ; No. 4 (16) With Nehru on his Tour ; No. 5 (17) Benaras : No. 6 (18) The Rulers and Rajahs of the South—Calcutta ; No. 7 (19) Prophets, saints, crusaders of the West ; No. 8 (20) Indo-China, Vietnam ; No. 9 (21) War in Indo-China ; No. 10 (22) London Season—Sculptor Epstein ; No. 11 (23) English Theatre ; No. 12 (24) Siam ; No. 13 (25) Sun-bathing ; No. 14 (26) Buddhism, Southern School ; No. 15 (27) Burma ; No. 16 (28) The Great U Po Sein and the Pwé Theatre ; No. 17 (29) The Englishwoman ; No. 18 (30) The Labour Party of Britain ; No. 19 (31) Malaya ; No. 20 (32) The Crown Colony of Singapore ; No. 21 (33) Her Britannic Majesty's Government ; Summary of 'Annual Abstract of Statistics for the U.K.' ; No. 22-23 (34-35) Hong Kong ; No. 24 (36) The Court of Chivalry.

VOLUME III, 1955 :
No. 1 (37) The Pope ; Nehru ; "The Song of Business" ; No. 2 (38) Macau ; No. 3-4 (39-40) The Artist in Modern Society ; No. 5-7 (41-43) Sarawak (Borneo) ; No. 8 (44) Costumes of Britain in the mid-XXth century ; No. 9-10 (45-46) Tokyo — Fuji — Kyoto ; No. 11 (47) Japanese Theatre ; No. 12-13 (48-49) Tokyo ; No. 14 (50) San Francisco ; No. 15 (51) Hollywood ; No. 16 (52) Brazilian Dancers ; Caribbean Dancers ; No. 17 (53) Coast-to-coast ; No. 18-22 (54-58) New York ; No. 23-24 (59-60) Washington D.C.

THE DRAWINGS IN "TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE" ARE DELIBERATELY SELECTED ONLY FROM THOSE WHICH WERE PUT ON PAPER "FROM LIFE" AND "IN ACTION"; THUS THE AUTHENTICITY OF DOCUMENTATION IS MAINTAINED. IN THE FEW CASES WHEN PAINTINGS OR COMPOSED DRAWINGS ARE INCLUDED, THEIR DIFFERENT NATURE IS STATED
sketch_descriptions: A sketch of a woman turned sideways holding a doll under her arm.
A detailed group sketch of multiple people sitting and interacting at tables, depicted in various poses and angles.
A portrait sketch of a person with long hair, head tilted and looking upwards.
A scene showing a busy street or pathway with a group of people walking; in the background are buildings including a pagoda-like structure labeled 'TOKYO - FUJI - KYOTO'.
A portrait sketch of a man's profile, captioned 'U.S.A. ELECTS PRESIDENT'.

pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle features a detailed index of topics, people, locations, and events covered in various issues along with a list of drawings accompanied by illustrations depicting people and scenes from different cultures and historical moments spanning 1953 to 1955.
